cards.title: SoftBank to Invest €75B in French Data Centers
cards.body: SoftBank plans to develop and operate up to 5 gigawatts of additional data center capacity in France. The investment signals major expansion in European AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Github Copilot Shifts to Token-Based Billing Model
cards.body: Microsoft's Github Copilot is moving away from free access with new token-based billing. Developers are expressing frustration over the end of the platform's golden age.

cards.title: Meta Developing AI-Powered Hardware Pendant
cards.body: Meta is reportedly building an AI pendant as part of broader hardware ambitions. The device reflects Meta's strategic push into AI-powered wearables.

cards.title: Google Launches Gemini Spark 24/7 Assistant
cards.body: Gemini Spark automates everyday tasks from inbox summaries to event planning. Google's 24/7 AI assistant offers practical utility but raises questions about its separate product positioning.

cards.title: Groq Raises $650M Pivoting to AI Inference
cards.body: AI chip startup Groq is securing $650 million in funding while shifting focus from hardware to inference optimization. The move comes after Nvidia's $20B not-acquisition of talent.

cards.title: Cognition's Devin Shouldn't Replace Human Coders
cards.body: Cognition CEO Scott Wu clarifies that AI coding agent Devin is designed to augment, not replace developers. The distinction highlights ongoing debate about AI's role in software engineering.

cards.title: XCENA Raises $135M Betting on Memory Bottleneck
cards.body: South Korean chip startup XCENA claims AI's real constraint is memory, not compute power. The $135M funding validates a contrarian approach to AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Glean Crosses $300M Revenue with AI Budgets
cards.body: Enterprise AI search startup Glean tripled annual revenue despite competition from tech giants. Cost-cutting has become its major selling point as enterprises optimize AI spending.

cards.title: Asana Acquires No-Code Agent Builder StackAI
cards.body: Asana is integrating StackAI into its AI workflow tools suite. The acquisition strengthens Asana's competitive position in enterprise AI automation.

cards.title: Internet Being Rebuilt for Machine Traffic
cards.body: AWS, Cloudflare, and others are redesigning infrastructure for AI agents moving from experiments to production. Cloud platforms are adapting for machine-generated internet traffic dominance.

cards.title: Erin Brockovich Targets Data Center Secrecy
cards.body: Environmental activist Erin Brockovich is investigating data center operations and transparency. Her campaign focuses on corporate accountability in AI infrastructure expansion.

cards.title: Tech CEOs Exhibit 'AI Psychosis,' Critics Say
cards.body: Box founder Aaron Levie argues company leaders making AI decisions don't understand affected jobs. ClickUp's 22% layoffs for AI agents exemplify the disconnect.

cards.title: Coders Refusing Work Without AI Tools
cards.body: Developers increasingly demand AI assistance, but research questions code quality improvements. The trend may create long-term technical and career challenges.

cards.title: Essential AI Terminology Glossary for 2026
cards.body: The AI boom has created an avalanche of new terms requiring clarification. A comprehensive glossary helps decode common words and phrases.

cards.title: Top Browser Alternatives to Chrome, Safari
cards.body: Alternative browsers are challenging Chrome and Safari dominance in 2026. The browser wars are intensifying with privacy and AI features as battlegrounds.

cards.title: Kiwibit AI Bird Feeder Connects Nature, Tech
cards.body: Kiwibit's smart feeder combines AI with bird tracking gamification. The device offers a novel way to engage with nature through app-based species collection.

cards.title: Github Copilot's Token-Based Billing Sparks Developer Backlash
cards.body: Microsoft's Github Copilot is shifting to a token-based billing model, marking the end of its free tier era. Developers are expressing frustration over the pricing changes for the AI coding assistant.

cards.title: Meta Reportedly Developing AI-Powered Hardware Pendant
cards.body: Meta is making significant bets on AI-powered hardware devices. The AI pendant represents the company's expanding vision for wearable AI technology.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark AI Assistant Automates Daily Tasks
cards.body: Google's 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark helps automate everyday tasks from inbox summaries to event planning. The tool proves useful but raises questions about why Google separated it as a distinct product.

cards.title: AI Code Generation May Sacrifice Quality for Speed
cards.body: Developers increasingly rely on AI to produce code faster, but research warns this approach may compromise code quality. The trend could create long-term problems for software systems.

cards.title: Essential AI Glossary: Understanding New Technology Terms
cards.body: The rise of AI has brought numerous new terms and slang to the technology landscape. This glossary breaks down important words and phrases you'll encounter in AI discussions.

cards.title: CEOs Suffer from AI Psychosis, Says Box Founder
cards.body: Box founder Aaron Levie warns that executives making AI decisions often lack understanding of actual job responsibilities. This 'AI psychosis' has led companies like ClickUp to cut 22% of their workforce for AI agents.

cards.title: Groq Raises $650M Pivoting Toward AI Inference
cards.body: Following Nvidia's $20B not-acquisition, chipmaker Groq is raising $650 million while shifting focus from hardware to AI inference optimization. The pivot targets the process of refining how AI models respond to prompts.

cards.title: Cognition's Devin AI Agent Won't Replace Human Coders
cards.body: Cognition CEO Scott Wu emphasizes that Devin, the first successful AI coding agent, is designed to assist rather than replace programmers. The distinction reflects growing concern about AI's role in workforce displacement.

cards.title: XCENA Bets AI Bottleneck Is Memory, Not Compute
cards.body: South Korean chip startup XCENA raised $135 million on the thesis that memory, not processing power, is AI's limiting factor. The bet challenges conventional wisdom about AI infrastructure requirements.

cards.title: Glean Triples Revenue as Budget-Cutting AI Goes Mainstream
cards.body: Enterprise AI search startup Glean crossed $300M in annual revenue despite competition from tech giants. Cost-reduction has become the company's major selling point in a shifting market.

cards.title: Cloud Infrastructure Rebuilt for AI Agent Traffic
cards.body: AWS, Cloudflare, and others are redesigning cloud infrastructure for AI agents moving from experiments to production. The shift anticipates machine-generated traffic replacing human users as the internet's primary consumer.

cards.title: Asana Acquires StackAI No-Code Agent Builder
cards.body: Asana incorporated the no-code agent-builder StackAI into its expanding suite of AI workflow tools. The acquisition strengthens Asana's position in enterprise AI automation.

cards.title: Pope's Encyclical Frames AI Ethics for Tech Leaders
cards.body: Pope Leo XIV's encyclical Magnifica Humanitas emphasizes that 'technology is never neutral' in addressing artificial intelligence. The document serves as a template for individuals and policymakers navigating the AI moment.

cards.title: Organizations Want Agentic AI But Lack Infrastructure
cards.body: Although 85% of organizations aim to become agentic within three years, 76% say their current operations can't support the transition. The gap reveals a critical disconnect between AI ambition and execution capabilities.

cards.title: AI Jobs Panic Lacks Evidence, Reality Check Shows
cards.body: Despite growing hysteria about AI threatening white-collar employment, evidence of actual job displacement remains scarce. The narrative surrounding AI's labor impact may be outpacing reality.

cards.title: LLMs Believe False Statements Despite Explicit Warnings
cards.body: Researchers found that large language models retain false beliefs even when explicitly warned they're incorrect. The finding highlights a significant limitation in how LLMs process contradictory information.
